meta.disclaimer: Platform positions are summarized from official party platforms and public statements. Always consult primary sources.

positions.title: Opposition to Palestinian State
positions.summary: Opposes the establishment of a Palestinian state west of the Jordan River. Advocates for Israeli security control over all territory between the river and the sea.

positions.title: Judicial Reform
positions.summary: Supports reforms to rebalance power between the Knesset and the judiciary, including changes to the override clause and judicial appointments committee.

positions.title: Free-Market Economy
positions.summary: Favors privatization, reducing business regulation, and encouraging foreign investment to stimulate growth.

positions.title: Settlement Expansion
positions.summary: Supports continued building in West Bank settlements and opposes settlement freezes as a precondition for negotiations.

positions.title: Strong Military Response in Gaza
positions.summary: Advocates for continued military pressure until Hamas is dismantled and all hostages are returned.

positions.title: Status Quo on Religion and State
positions.summary: Generally maintains the status quo arrangement on religious affairs, including Shabbat observance laws, as part of coalition agreements.

parties.name: BeYahad (Together)
parties.topline:
Implement a "New Agreement" that fully changes the book on how the government is run, and how it works for the benefit of Israel's citizens and Israel's standing.
To build a functioning government that works for its citizens, we need to completely overhaul it.

positions.title: Consitutional Reform
positions.summary:
Create a Constitution in line with the principles of the Declaration of Independence
Term limits for Prime Ministers: Limited to 8 consecutive years

positions.title: Commission of Inquiry
positions.summary: Open a non-partisan, apolitical State Commission of Inquiry into the events of October 7th and the conduct of the state afterwards.

positions.title: Judicial Reform
positions.summary: Limiting and formalizing the courts' abilities of Judicial Review and striking basic laws, subject to broad consensus.

positions.title: Meeting the IDF Manpower Needs
positions.summary: Recruit 20,000 additional combat soldiers

positions.title: Improving the education system
positions.summary:
Improve quality of public education to meet the level of private schools
Improve teacher salaries
Extending the school day until 4:00 PM, and increasing classes, athletics, and provide a hot meal.

positions.title: Improving the education system
positions.summary: Completely revamp the Ministry of Education

positions.title: Fighting organized crime
positions.summary: Focus on rooting out organized crime. Identify the top 3 families and top 100 criminals and go after them directly

positions.title: Improve the public service
positions.summary: Improve the quality of the public service, through the use of KPIs.

positions.title: Move authorities to local governments
positions.summary: Decentralize powers and give more authority to local regional governments

positions.title: Fighting the cost of living
positions.summary: Lower the cost of food by 30% within a year, by dismantling closed industries and opening Israel to foreign suppliers

positions.title: Draft Equality
positions.summary:
National Service Law (ie military service) will apply to all Israeli citizens, regardless of ethnicity, religious community, or gender. Laws that permit draft evasion will be repealed.
Draft dodgers or draft evaders will have benefits removed, specifically:
- Kindergarten subsidies
- Property tax discounts
- Bituach Leumi discounts and student status
- Child support benefits
Estimated savings: 4.4 billion NIS

positions.title: Creating a core curriculum
positions.summary: The state will only fund schools in the state education stream, which will be obligated to teach the core curriculum, making up a minimum of 60% of education hours.

positions.title: Benefits only to those who embrace Israeli society
positions.summary: Benefits and government funding will only go to citizens and communities that work, enlist, and participate in the state education system.

positions.title: Improving Israel's position internationally
positions.summary: Actively combat international anti-Israel sentiment and work to create a positive brand. Improve regional relationships and end international political isolation. Build regional alliances.

positions.title: Jews around the world
positions.summary: Recognize that Israel's political position directly impacts the diaspora

positions.title: Fighting crime in the Negev
positions.summary: Recognize that the Negev is in a state of emergency, and bring the Shin Bet to fight crime.

positions.title: Building the Periphery
positions.summary: Increase settlement of the Negev and the North, and clamp down on illegal construction

positions.title: Combatting incitement
positions.summary: Monitor the education system to make sure that incitement against the state isn't being taught

positions.title: Fighting polygamy
positions.summary: Enforce bans on polygamy, including closing loopholes with the PA, and ensuring that Bituach Leumi isn't providing illegal benefits

positions.title: Bringing the Beduins into Israeli society
positions.summary: Build the connection between the Bedouin community and the state, with economic stimulus, education, and sanctions.

positions.title: Improve Miluimnik Benefits
positions.summary:
Active Miluimnikim will get: 
- 1 million shekels towards their first home 
- Free daycare, free after-school programs, free summer school
- 50% discount on all state service fees, including utilities, taxes, and car tests
*Defined as reservists who served 20 days in reserves over 3 years, or 15 days in combat rolls

positions.title: Improve Veteran Benefits
positions.summary:
Veterans (including those who did National Service/Sheirut Leumi) will get:
- Priority in the housing lottery
- Fully funded bachelor's degree (combat veterans will also get a Masters degree)
- Free bus/train pass for 3 years (combat veterans will get for 5 years)
- Fully funded after-school and summer schools
- Daycare for 1000 NIS/month

positions.title: Fight corruption though wasteful political spending
positions.summary: "Coalition Funds" (government funding for niche party priorities that is promised to get parties to join a coalition) will be cancelled. Useless government ministries will be closed. Estimated savings: 4.4 billion NIS Move away from political appointments to professional appointments.

positions.title: Building the North
positions.summary: Define Metula, Shlomi, and Kiryat Shemoneh as a National Reconstruction Area, with the goal of doubling the population (relative to before the war) within 10 years.

positions.title: Encouraging business in the North
positions.summary:
Create a temporary (4 years) tax exempt zone in the North near Kiryat Shemonah:
- No corporate tax for new local industries, tech companies, and defense companies
- 50% discount on property taxes for businesses
- Grants for expansion
- Subsidize high salaries

positions.title: Encouraging living in the North
positions.summary:
Encourage return and new settlement though economic benefits:
- No income tax up to 1 million shekels (for 4 years)
- Housing loan of 400,00 shekels for academics and professionals after 7 years of residence

positions.title: Connecting the North with the rest of Israel
positions.summary:
Improve transit services, healthcare services, and transit access in Kiryat Shemona:
- Build a protected emergency room
- Build public transit access
- Provide subsidized flights at 99 shekels to Tel Aviv Ben Gurion

positions.title: Improve rehabilitation services for injured and disabled veterans
positions.summary:
Create a National Rehabilitation Directorate under the Prime Minister's Office, to complement the activities of the Rehabilitation Division of the Ministry of Defense by creating a coordinating body for the relevant offices in Finance, Health, Housing, and Justice. Provide lawyers at the expense of the state to every injured veteran, from the first meeting. Unblock bureaucracy around rehabilitation, including making all requests and forms digital and streamlined.
Remove roadblocks from injured veterans returning to the workforce without losing support and rights. Give local authorities and communities funding and tools to support disabled veterans. Publish benchmarks and scores to show if the rehabilitation system is improving.

positions.title: Improving mental health access and capacity
positions.summary:
Expand access to mental health supports, by increasing the number of students in mental health fields and incentivising joining the public health services. Also create tools for self care and self support.
Lowering prices for mental health care for youth. Strengthen resilience centers and mental health first aid centers.

positions.title: Housing for disabled veterans
positions.summary: Create a new supportive housing path for wounded and disabled service veterans

positions.title: Improve Private and Public Transit
positions.summary: Invest into public transit, increase the amount of high speed rail, and improve bus reliability. Create a national plan to reduce congestion.

positions.title: Get local authorities involved in transit
positions.summary: Move responsibilities and authorities around transit to local regional authorities

positions.title: Supporting the elderly
positions.summary: Increase old age pensions by indexing them to the average salary. Doubling the pensions of retirees making below minimum wage, and gradually increasing pensions for those just above it.

positions.title: Housing for veterans
positions.summary: Expand the subsidized rental program by 250,000 units, and build a new city in the Merkaz specifically for young veterans.

positions.title: Improving access to housing
positions.summary: Allow residents in the subsidized rental program to buy their apartment at cost, minus rent paid, after 10 years.

positions.title: Public Transit on Shabbat
positions.summary: Allow public transit on shabbat, based on the decision by the local regional authority.

positions.title: Civil Marriage
positions.summary: Break the Rabbanut monopoly on marriage, and facilitate civil marriages

positions.title: Annexation of Gaza & Jewish Resettlement
positions.summary: Advocates for full Israeli annexation of the Gaza Strip and the establishment of Jewish settlements there following military operations.

parties.name: Yisrael Beiteinu
parties.topline: Our government will reconstruct the damaged regions and heal the rifts in Israeli society. It will only contain Zionist parties, and make a modern, democratic, and liberal Jewish state, while also encouraging Aliyah and building ties with the Diaspora.

positions.title: Commission of Inquiry
positions.summary: Establish a full State Commission of Inquiry to examine the failures that lead up to October 7th. The Commission will have a mandate to determine responsibility in the Political, Military, and Intelligence levels. The Commission with be authorized to critique failures and bring justice

positions.title: Draft Equality
positions.summary: Pass a new law that mandates conscription and service of every Israeli at the age of 18, regardless of religion or community. Both military and civilian (healthcare, police, firefighting, ect) will qualify.

positions.title: Benefits only for those who draft
positions.summary:
Anyone who doesn't draft/serve will be barred from rights and benefits, including: 
- Housing and tax discounts
- Childcare benefits
- The right to work in the public service
- Affirmative action to get into university

positions.title: Penalties for draft avoidance
positions.summary:
Draft dodgers will be subject to: 
- Criminal Records
- Losing the right to vote
- Losing the right to leave the country

positions.title: Constitutional Reform
positions.summary:
Create a Constitution, in line with the work prepared by professionals here. Elements include:
  • The largest party in the Knesset gets first chance to form a government
  • Budgets failing will no longer trigger elections
  • Basic laws become constitutional laws
  • Limits to number of ministers
  • Term limits to the Prime Minister
  • The Government has the right to independant legal representation
  • Repealing the "Norwegian Law", where Ministers can opt out of the Knesset and be replaced by other party members
  • Reform the Judicial System
  • Obligate every citizen to serve.

positions.title: Gaza Strip
positions.summary:
Maintain freedom of IDF action in Gaza. 
End Israeli responsibility for Gaza, and stop suppying water, electricity, and maanging the border. 
Transfer responsibility to Egypt, and if Egypt doesn't agree, to the Palestinians to do whatever they want, without out management.

positions.title: West Bank
positions.summary: Work with Jordan to increase security and economic development for Palestinians. Transfer Area A to Jordan, and keep areas B and C.

positions.title: Cost of Living
positions.summary: Reduce regulation, downsize government ministries, increasing competition

positions.title: Core curriculum in Education
positions.summary:
Mandated core curriculum of: Math, English, Technology, Civics, and Tanach. 
Institutions that don't meet requirements will not be funded.

positions.title: Increasing teachers salaries
positions.summary: Salary increases for teachers will be directly deposited to their bank accounts

positions.title: Reducing Rabbanut Overhead
positions.summary: Only one Chief Rabbi of Israel, instead of the current 2. One City Rabbi for each authority. Abolishing religious councils and make religious services be a department in municipalities.

positions.title: Breaking Rabbanut Monopoly on Marriage
positions.summary: Legislate civil marriage and divorce

positions.title: Breaking Rabbanut Monopoly on Conversion
positions.summary: Legislate the Nissim Report on conversion, which would move conversion away from the Rabbanut to an independant (Orthodox) body

positions.title: Public Transit on Shabbat
positions.summary: Allowing local authorities to descide whether to offer public transit on shabbat

positions.title: Aliyah and the Diaspora
positions.summary: Create a committee to encourage Aliyah. Work with the Diaspora to make Aliyah easy and seamless.

positions.title: New Bodies to Fight Crime
positions.summary: Make a National Crime Headquarters against organized crime, and set up 3 new specialized courts for serious crime: The North, Lod, the South

positions.title: New Crime Fighting Legislation
positions.summary: New legislation to allow for the administrative arrest of heads of criminal organizations, and to permit the use of spyware to catch them

positions.title: Palestinian Statehood
positions.summary: No Palestinian State. End the Oslo Accords. Transfer Gaza to Egypt and Area A of the West Bank to Jordan. Do not trust the PA.
